prompts: A caching mechanism can be effectively presented by explaining the structure of cache layers, beginning with the primary data storage or database as the source of truth. Cache layers are strategically placed to improve retrieval efficiency, often near the application layer, to store frequently accessed data, reducing the need to query the primary storage repeatedly. The system uses algorithms to determine what data to cache, when to expire it, and how to update it, balancing between memory usage and retrieval speed. This layered approach enhances performance by minimizing data access times and server load.
